meanings.definition: processing that involves blowing a gas
exhale hard
examples: blow on the soup to cool it down

meanings.definition: be blowing or storming
examples: The wind blew from the West

meanings.definition: free of obstruction by blowing air through
be in motion due to some air or water current
examples: The leaves were blowing in the wind
the boat drifted on the lake
The sailboat was adrift on the open sea
the shipwrecked boat drifted away from the shore

meanings.definition: make a sound as if blown
shape by blowing
make a mess of, destroy or ruin
examples: I botched the dinner and we had to eat out
the pianist screwed up the difficult passage in the second movement

meanings.definition: spend thoughtlessly; throw away
examples: He wasted his inheritance on his insincere friends
You squandered the opportunity to get and advanced degree

meanings.definition: spend lavishly or wastefully on
examples: He blew a lot of money on his new home theater

meanings.definition: sound by having air expelled through a tube
play or sound a wind instrument
provide sexual gratification through oral stimulation
cause air to go in, on, or through
cause to move by means of an air current
examples: The wind blew the leaves around in the yard

meanings.definition: spout moist air from the blowhole
leave; informal or rude
lay eggs
examples: certain insects are said to blow

meanings.definition: cause to be revealed and jeopardized
examples: The double agent was blown by the other side

meanings.definition: show off
allow to regain its breath
melt, break, or become otherwise unusable
burst suddenly
